UK Corporate Compliance

Companies House Compliance

All statutory filings including confirmation statements, annual returns, and changes. Our compliance specialists ensure your company remains in good standing with Companies House.

Get a QuoteBook Consultation

Complete Companies House Management

Companies House compliance is essential for maintaining your company's legal status and avoiding penalties. Our team manages all statutory filings, ensuring you never miss a deadline and your company remains in good standing.

We handle everything from incorporation to ongoing compliance, including confirmation statements, changes to directors and shareholders, share capital changes, and voluntary strike-off applications. We also provide guidance on director responsibilities and corporate governance.

Key Filing Deadlines

  • Confirmation Statement: Within 14 days of anniversary
  • Annual Accounts: 9 months after year-end (private)
  • Director Changes: Within 14 days of change
  • Shareholder Changes: Within 14 days of change
  • Registered Office Change: Within 14 days of change
  • PSC Register Updates: Within 14 days of change

Our Companies House Services

Company Formation

Complete company incorporation service including name availability check, memorandum and articles, and registration with Companies House.

Confirmation Statements

Preparation and filing of annual confirmation statements with accurate company information and PSC register updates.

Annual Accounts

Preparation and filing of statutory accounts in full compliance with UK GAAP and Companies House requirements.

Change Filings

All change notifications including director appointments/resignations, shareholder changes, and registered office updates.

Share Capital Management

Allotment and transfer of shares, share class changes, and capital reduction applications with proper documentation.

Strike-off & Restoration

Voluntary strike-off applications and company restoration services for dissolved companies.

People with Significant Control (PSC)

Since 2016, UK companies must maintain a register of People with Significant Control and file this information with Companies House. A PSC is anyone who owns more than 25% of shares, voting rights, or has significant influence over the company.

Who is a PSC?

  • • Owns more than 25% of shares
  • • Holds more than 25% of voting rights
  • • Can appoint or remove majority of directors
  • • Has significant influence or control

Our PSC Services

  • • PSC register maintenance
  • • PSC identification and verification
  • • PSC statement filings
  • • RLE (Relevant Legal Entity) registrations

Late Filing Penalties

Companies House imposes automatic penalties for late filings. These penalties increase with the length of delay and can be substantial. We track all deadlines and ensure timely filing to avoid these penalties.

Late Confirmation Statement

  • • Up to 1 month late: £150
  • • Up to 3 months late: £375
  • • Up to 6 months late: £750
  • • Over 6 months late: £1,500

Late Accounts (Private Company)

  • • Up to 1 month late: £150
  • • Up to 3 months late: £375
  • • Up to 6 months late: £750
  • • Over 6 months late: £1,500

*Penalties double for companies that are late for two consecutive years.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the difference between confirmation statement and annual return?

The confirmation statement replaced the annual return in 2016. It confirms that Companies House has the correct information about your company. It must be filed annually within 14 days of your incorporation anniversary.

What happens if I miss a filing deadline?

Companies House automatically imposes penalties for late filings. These increase with the length of delay. We track all deadlines and file on time to avoid penalties. If you have missed a deadline, we can help mitigate the situation.

Can you help with company restoration?

Yes. If your company has been struck off the register, we can assist with restoration applications. This involves court proceedings and administrative restoration, depending on the circumstances and time elapsed.

Do I need a company secretary?

Since April 2008, private companies are not required to have a company secretary. However, many companies choose to appoint one for administrative support. We can provide company secretary services if required.

Ensure Your Company Compliance

Let us handle your Companies House filings while you focus on running your business.
